Hey, welcome back to the next lesson for unconscious communication. Let's dive right in. There are two minds, so to speak, that make up our overall brain, our overall mind. You have the conscious mind and you have the unconscious mind. Now the conscious mind is essentially what you focus on your conscious awareness, right? What you're thinking about, what you're looking at. If you read something, you're focusing specifically on that's your conscious awareness, right? Then you have your unconscious mind, which is basically represents your autonomic nervous system, your long-term memories, the things that are the deepest recesses, so to speak, right? Here's my question before I dive into this. Which of these two minds do you think is more powerful? You think the unconscious mind is more powerful or you think the conscious mind is more powerful? Which one do you think has more power, more processing ability? Okay, well that's a fair guess because it makes sense that the conscious mind would have a heck of a lot of processing power, right? It's what you're talking about, what you're thinking about, you know, when you look at something and you're processing and analyzing it, it's all conscious, right? But guess what? You're totally wrong. Your conscious mind has a sliver of processing power when compared to your unconscious mind, which is infinitely more powerful. Correct. Your unconscious mind is infinitely more powerful. See, here's the deal. Your unconscious mind is what allows you essentially to maintain all aspects of your physiology the same. We call that homeostasis to remain the same. Your heart rate, your pulse, your breathing, your basic temperature, right? Because homeostasis, this ability to move through the world and have all systems of your body functioning perfectly without even thinking about them, that's all done or accomplished through your unconscious mind through the autonomic nervous system. In addition, the unconscious mind also has all your memories. That's where everything is stored. For instance, right now, as you focus on me, that's where your conscious awareness is, right? You're not my voice, seeing my body, and maybe you also pick up any loud noises in the background, maybe something directly in front of you, but most of your conscious awareness right now is directed towards my voice and my image, right? Well guess what? You're picking up, you're taking in a heck of a lot more data than that. In fact, what you don't feel is the air on your skin, the feeling of the seat pressing into your bottom, all the things, all the sensory data, there's massive amount of data coming at you that your conscious mind couldn't possibly focus on. It doesn't have enough processing power. So what happens is, because it has a limited amount of processing power, it chooses to focus on what it deems important, which right now is this training. So you're focusing on me and you're deleting out all this other information around you. So it comes in three of five senses, your conscious mind deletes it out, but your unconscious mind captures everything. It then takes that information and stores it in your brain in the form of patterns. And we form these patterns that allow us to move through the world in pretty much of a seamless fashion. For instance, as I go up to a door, if I haven't opened up the door before, I don't say, stop, wait a second. It's a piece of wood with a knob on the end. What is this? Your brain says, this is a door. We've seen all these doors before. It's called generalizing. And that is stored in your unconscious mind. You generalize up these rectangular objects with knobs, or called doors. And if I'm in a non-combat situation, it's safe to open up the door. There's not going to be a bomb going off, you understand? So we are able to move through the world using these patterns of behavior that are stored in our unconscious mind. Conversely, when you have something like with PTSD, so that's actually been to war, you have to see them open up. They're like, because they know it's not so safe. They have a different pattern formed. But that's how we move through life. We don't look at every crack in the sidewalk. We don't have to worry about every single door. We generalize the world around us. And we form patterns, and they're all stored in our unconscious mind. Now here's the deal. These patterns are the gut reactions that we feel. These gut feelings, like when you sense that someone is sharp as a tack, enthusiastic, an expert in their field, what's happening is, is that in those first four seconds, it's the unconscious mind that comes into play to make that initial judgment. Is this person sharp? Are they on the ball? Are they an expert? Are they enthusiastic? And he actually uses a pattern, a stored pattern, and says, what does this person match? Are they an expert? Or are they a novice? Are they sharp? Or are they dull? Are they enthusiastic? Or are they seem like they're bored, basically, right? And based on which pattern you hit, bam, that's how you are perceived. Now, if you perceive the right way, meaning you're sharp on the ball, expert, they defer to you. They actually give you control of the conversation because they say, you know, this is an expert. They're a person worth listening to who can help me achieve my goals, help me get what I want in life. That's the key. That comes from the unconscious mind based on a stored pattern. But here's what happens. As soon as those four seconds are essentially lapsed, the conscious mind then kicks into overdrive and starts hanging on every word of the salesperson, that every body language is saying, was I fooled? Is this person really an expert? Are they that sharp? And if the salesperson screws up or is found out to be a fraud, guess what? The prospect tries to take back control of the sale and you're done. All the goodwill that you've gotten to this point, any rapport that you've built will be destroyed and you can't close anyone. That's why it's so absolutely crucial that you learn how to communicate at the highest level, not just for the first few seconds, but through the entire encounter. And that is why the straight line works so well. Get it? Because we focus both on the conscious and unconscious aspects of sales and communication for the entire sales encounter. That's the deal. Now, one thing that you need to know, as powerful and effective as the straight line is, it breaks down in the absence of one crucial element. Namely, the ability to manage your physiological, emotional state, how you feel in the moment. In the next module, that's what we're going to learn. This ability to manage your state, you show up in the right state. You have absolute certainty, confidence, clarity. Once you know how to do that, that's the X factor that gives the straight line system its gasoline, this jet engine to run basically. So that's the next module. Before we go there, I have a short quiz for you to end up unconscious communication and believe me, you're doing great here. So finish it up and then let's rock on and I'll see you in the next module.
